outliers.title: The Most Dangerous Snacks Ever Sold

transcript_analysis.title: The Most Dangerous Snacks Ever Sold
transcript_analysis.transcript_preview: Kind: captions Language: en In September 2023, a 14-year-old boy ate a single tortilla chip, then suddenly collapsed and soon after his life ended. Pocky's one chip challenge had been sold in seasonal versions since 2016. The concept was simple. One tortilla chip dusted with Carolina Reaper and Naga Viper pepper powder sold individually in a small coffin-shaped box. outliers.title: 8 UK Custard Brands to Skip(And 2 That Are Actually Worth it)

transcript_analysis.title: 8 UK Custard Brands to Skip(And 2 That Are Actually Worth it)
transcript_analysis.transcript_preview: Kind: captions Language: en Most custard sold in UK supermarkets contains little or no real egg. It is modified starch, vegetable oil, flavoring and coloring. Sold as a traditional British dessert that was originally built around eggs and cream. I tested 10 UK custard brands. Eight failed, only two were real custard. outliers.title: Watch how Senators voted to jail Canadians for “denialism”

transcript_analysis.title: Watch how Senators voted to jail Canadians for “denialism”
transcript_analysis.transcript_preview: Kind: captions Language: en A Senate committee introduced an 11th hour amendment that would jail Canadians for so much as downplaying the Indian residential school system. It was immediately voted down by the full Senate and there are signs that if the amendment had made it back to the House of Commons, they would have voted it down, too.
